summary: Small configure.py-only PR that attempted to mark several already-free/nonmatching object files as Matching in the build configuration, plus removed one NonMatching ft_3C61.c entry. The PR was closed without merge; a comment from PsiLupan says the handling was moved to a separate PR.
what_changed: Removed the Object(NonMatching, "melee/ft/ft_3C61.c") entry from the MatchingFor list.
smart_moves: Kept the change narrowly scoped to configure.py, avoiding unrelated source edits while only updating build/link matching status.
Grouped several 'free' files into one linking-status sweep, making the matching-progress update easy to audit from the diff.
Separated this handling into its own PR according to the visible issue comment, which is useful for isolating link-status churn from functional decomp changes.
decomp_lessons: When an object file is confirmed linkable/matching without source changes, the decomp progress update may be just a configure.py Object(NonMatching) to Object(Matching) flip.
Matching-status-only PRs should be easy to review because they encode a build/link verification result rather than new decompiled logic.

If a NonMatching object entry is removed instead of converted to Matching, future readers should verify whether the object was moved, became covered elsewhere, or was intentionally no longer linked; this PR slice does not provide the reason.
naming_conventions: configure.py uses Object(Matching, "path/to/file.c") and Object(NonMatching, "path/to/file.c") to encode per-object matching/link status.
Player files in this area use lowercase pl prefixes such as melee/pl/plattack.c, melee/pl/plbonus.c, and melee/pl/plbonuslib.c.
Fighter common action files use ftCo_ prefixes, e.g. ftCo_CaptureKirby.c and ftCo_ItemParasolFall.c.
Popo/Nana fighter files use ftPp_ and ftNn_ prefixes; the diff includes ftPp_SpecialS.c and nearby ftNn_Init.c / ftNn_Unk0.c.
Sysdolphin baselib files are listed under sysdolphin/baselib/ with lowercase module filenames such as bytecode.c, class.c, and hash.c.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Primary tactic was build-list promotion: flip known-good objects from NonMatching to Matching in configure.py once they can be linked as matching.
No assembly-level tricks, function rewrites, register-allocation workarounds, or source matching tactics are shown in the slice.
Because only configure.py changed, the evidence indicates these were already solved/free files rather than newly matched functions in this PR.
review_feedback: No formal reviews or inline review comments are present in the provided slice.
Visible issue comment from PsiLupan: "Handling this in a separate PR."
The PR was closed without merge, so future researchers should look for the follow-up/separate PR for the final accepted handling.
follow_up_queries: Find the separate PR referenced by PsiLupan's comment for the final accepted handling of these free file link-status changes.
Check whether melee/ft/ft_3C61.c was moved, linked elsewhere, or intentionally removed from the configure.py object list.
Verify the eventual matching status of plattack.c, plbonus.c, ftchangeparam.c, ftPp_SpecialS.c, and sysdolphin/baselib/bytecode.c on master after this closed PR.
